chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. Misc. Applications No.332 and 177 of 2023 Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Syed Fiaz ul Hassan Shah Applicant:- The State/ Anti-Narcotics Force through Mr. Mohsin Ali Khan, Special Prosecutor, ANF. Respondents:- Bilal Ahmed [in Cr Misc. Application No.332/2023] Muhammad Ikram [in Cr. Misc. Application No.177/2023] through M/s. Mallag Assa Dashti and Palwasha Tareen, advocates. Date of hearing:- 11.03.2026 =============== MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: These applications have been filed for cancellation of bail granted to the respondents in Crime No.42/2022, under sections 6, 9 Sr. 3(C) CNS Amendment Act, 2022, registered at PS ANF, Clifton, Karachi vide impugned orders dated 19.01.2023 and 05.01.2023. 2. The main contention of the learned Special Prosecutor ANF is that huge quantity of narcotics viz. 100 kgs Charas was recovered from respondents, who are Excise Police officials but the Court has granted them bail. 3. We have seen the impugned orders. It appears that the trial Court, considering presence of a USB and report of SHO of PS Hub City that on the day of incident, a quarrel had taken place between the Excise Police and ANF over the incident, has granted bail to respondents. Further, it is stated that information of the Charas was communicated to two agencies viz. ANF and Excise Police who reached the spot after one and other, and spatted with each other over the fact that as to who should be the complainant. These disputed facts, according to learned trial Court, had made the case as one of further inquiry. But in any case, the order was passed more than three years back and still it appears that ANF has failed to conclude the case.

These disputed facts, according to learned trial Court, had made the case as one of further inquiry. But in any case, the order was passed more than three years back and still it appears that ANF has failed to conclude the case. In the circumstances, we are not inclined to allow these applications and dismiss the same. The Cr. Misc. Applications are accordingly disposed of in above terms. JUDGE HANIF JUDGE
